title: Today's Markets Brief: Asia stocks mixed amid Iran uncertainty; Nikkei, KOSPI slide from records
summary: A quick look at the day's market news, with Asia stocks mixed, Nvidia's AI chip plans, and more.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the market news. Asia stocks are mixed today, with investors keeping a close eye on the situation in Iran. The Nikkei and KOSPI are both sliding from their recent record highs.

According to a report from Investing.com, Asia stocks are mixed amid Iran uncertainty. The Nikkei and KOSPI, which had recently hit record highs, are now taking a bit of a breather. I'm watching to see how this geopolitical uncertainty plays out.

In other news, Nvidia's CEO is saying that the company can meet demand for AI chips, despite ongoing supply constraints. This is interesting because Nvidia has been a major player in the AI space, and their chips are in high demand. The CEO's comments are a bit of a relief, I think, for investors who have been worried about the company's ability to keep up with demand.

I'm also seeing that Nvidia's CEO is confident in the company's ability to meet AI chip demand. That's a positive sign for the company, and it's likely to be seen as a good thing by investors. On the other hand, the mixed Asia stocks and sliding Nikkei and KOSPI are a reminder that there's still a lot of uncertainty out there.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
